# Brief: D3 — PR #939 review response + comment trim

## Task

Two sub-tasks, two separate commits, in order:

**(1) Address the operator's two review comments on PR #939:**

- `packages/1-framework/1-core/config/src/contract-source-types.ts` — the
  `ContractSourceFormat` doc comment was rewritten without need. Restore the original
  one-liner verbatim: `/** Lets format-aware tooling avoid file-extension sniffing and opaque loader introspection. */`
- `packages/1-framework/2-authoring/psl-parser/src/interpret.ts` — reviewer asks
  "`extends ContractSourceProvider`?" on `PslInterpretCapable`. The union itself cannot
  be extended; the directive resolves to: `PslInterpretCapable extends
  PslContractSourceProvider` (imported from `@prisma-next/config/config-types`),
  dropping the duplicated `readonly sourceFormat: 'psl'` member. Simplify the guard
  signature accordingly — `source is PslInterpretCapable` now suffices (the capability
  carries the full provider shape); drop the `ContractSourceProvider &` intersection
  from the predicate and anywhere tests construct it. Update type tests to pin the
  narrowed type still exposes `load` / `inputs` alongside `interpret`.

**(2) Trim code comments across the whole PR diff** (`git diff origin/main...HEAD --
packages/`). Audit every comment the PR adds. **Remove** comments that:

- narrate implementation step-by-step (doc comments included),
- convey information obvious from the function signature or type definition,
- are inline comments that simply tell what the next line does,
- explain how TypeScript, a database, an external library, or a protocol works,
- refer to transient artifacts (`projects/**`, dispatch briefs), removed code, or
  overruled decisions.

**Keep only** comments that explain a non-obvious reason for the code's existence.

If, after trimming, a doc comment describes behavior in only SOME cases rather than
the function/type as a whole, convert it to an inline comment on the relevant lines —
then re-evaluate whether it survives the same rules.

Apply the rules honestly to every comment in the diff — including doc comments D1/D2
wrote (e.g. the `OpaqueContractSourceProvider` block: the sentence explaining TS
narrowing mechanics is "how TypeScript works"; the design intent — narrowing flows
through capability guards only — is a non-obvious reason and may survive, likely
shortened; judge each sentence separately). Test-file comments in the diff are in
scope too.

## Scope

**In:** files touched by PR #939 under `packages/` (config + psl-parser, src + test).
**Out:** everything else; no signature changes beyond sub-task (1); no new tests
except adjustments forced by (1); do not touch `projects/**`.

## Completed when

- [ ] Original `ContractSourceFormat` one-liner restored verbatim.
- [ ] `PslInterpretCapable extends PslContractSourceProvider`; no duplicated
      discriminant; guard predicate is `source is PslInterpretCapable`; type tests
      pin `load`/`inputs`/`interpret` all reachable after narrowing.
- [ ] Every comment in the PR diff either survives the keep-rule or is removed;
      conversions per the some-cases rule applied.
- [ ] Gates green: `pnpm --filter @prisma-next/config test`,
      `pnpm --filter @prisma-next/psl-parser test`, both package lints + typechecks,
      `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint:deps`. Zero new casts.

## Standing instruction

Stay focused on the goal; control scope. Do NOT reply to, or resolve, any GitHub
review threads — the orchestrator handles none and the operator handles all.

## Operational metadata

- **Time-box:** 45 min. Overrun → halt and surface.
- **Halt conditions:** the `extends` change breaks an in-repo consumer in a way that
  needs new design (surface, don't improvise); a comment's keep/remove call is
  genuinely ambiguous after applying the rules — batch such cases and surface them in
  the report rather than guessing silently.
